name: Chequilla from Pinilla de Molina — circular through the Alto Tajo Natural Park
name_original: Chequilla desde Pinilla de Molina — circular por el Parque Natural del Alto Tajo
region_intro: Touring cycling around Pinilla De Molina offers routes through the rugged landscapes of the Alto Tajo Natural Park. The region is characterized by deep canyons, extensive pine forests, and high plateaus, providing varied terrain for cyclists. Routes often feature significant elevation changes and remote sections, ideal for those seeking challenging rides. The area's natural beauty includes viewpoints and unique geological formations. Best touring cycling routes around Pinilla De Molina The most popular touring cycling route is Chequilla from Pinilla de Molina — circular through the Alto Tajo Natural Park, a 24.3 miles (39.0 km) trail that takes 2 hours 53 minutes to complete. This difficult route explores the natural park's diverse scenery. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Alto Tajo Park and Montañas Vacías Loop, a difficult 38.8 miles (62.4 km) path. This extensive loop traverses the natural park and the remote "Empty Mountains" region. Local touring cyclists also love the Molina de Aragón – The towers of Chequilla loop from ZEC Montes de Picaza, a 65.1 miles (104.7 km) trail leading through protected natural areas, often completed in about 7 hours 30 minutes. Touring cycling around Pinilla De Molina is defined by challenging climbs, remote natural park landscapes, and scenic viewpoints. The network offers options primarily for advanced touring bike routes, with some moderate trails available. The routes in Pinilla De Molina are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.8 stars from over 4 reviews. More than 70 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Pinilla De Molina's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available around Pinilla De Molina?
A: There are 12 touring cycling routes documented around Pinilla De Molina on komoot. These routes primarily explore the rugged landscapes of the Alto Tajo Natural Park.

Q: What is the overall difficulty level of touring cycling routes in Pinilla De Molina?
A: The touring cycling routes in Pinilla De Molina are generally challenging. Out of 12 routes, 11 are rated as difficult, and only one is moderate. There are no easy routes, indicating that the area is best suited for experienced cyclists seeking significant elevation changes and remote sections.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on touring cycling routes in Pinilla De Molina?
A: You can expect varied and rugged terrain, characterized by deep canyons, extensive pine forests, and high plateaus. Routes often feature significant elevation changes, making for demanding but rewarding rides through the Alto Tajo Natural Park and the remote "Empty Mountains" region.

Q: Are there any easy touring cycling routes suitable for beginners or families?
A: The touring cycling routes around Pinilla De Molina are predominantly difficult, with only one moderate option available. This suggests the area is more suited for experienced cyclists. Families or beginners might find the terrain and elevation changes quite challenging. The moderate route, &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/19813147" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Estrecho del Horcajo Viewpoint – Laguna de Taravilla loop from Peralejos de las Truchas&lt;/a&gt;, is 25.8 km long and still involves some climbing.

Q: Are there many circular touring cycling routes in the area?
A: Yes, several routes around Pinilla De Molina are designed as loops, allowing you to start and end in the same location. Examples include the popular &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1190924563" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Chequilla from Pinilla de Molina — circular through the Alto Tajo Natural Park&lt;/a&gt; and the extensive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1190953442" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alto Tajo Park and Montañas Vacías Loop&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What can I see along the touring cycling routes around Pinilla De Molina?
A: The routes offer access to stunning natural features and viewpoints within the Alto Tajo Natural Park. You can encounter unique geological formations like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2094395" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;The towers of Chequilla&lt;/a&gt;, visit the scenic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6268434" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Estrecho del Horcajo Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt;, or pass by the tranquil &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6498158" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Laguna de Taravilla&lt;/a&gt;. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5430933" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Salto de Poveda Waterfall&lt;/a&gt; is another notable natural monument in the vicinity.

Q: Are there any specific landmarks or points of interest to look out for?
A: Beyond the natural beauty, you can discover interesting landmarks such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1121501" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Suspension bridge over the River Tajo&lt;/a&gt;. The charming village of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5167073" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Checa&lt;/a&gt; is also a highlight that some routes pass through, offering a glimpse into local life.

Q: What do other touring cyclists enjoy the most about touring cycling in Pinilla De Molina?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.8 stars from over 4 reviews. Reviewers often praise the challenging climbs, the remote and unspoiled natural park landscapes, and the scenic viewpoints that offer breathtaking vistas of the region's unique geological formations.

Q: Are there any long-distance touring cycling routes for multi-day trips?
A: Yes, for those seeking longer adventures, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40005462" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Molina de Aragón – The towers of Chequilla loop from ZEC Montes de Picaza&lt;/a&gt; is a significant route. At 104.7 km, it typically takes around 7 hours 30 minutes to complete, making it suitable for a challenging day ride or a segment of a multi-day tour through protected natural areas.

Q: What is the best time of year for touring cycling in Pinilla De Molina?
A: Given the high plateaus and potentially remote sections, spring and autumn are generally the most pleasant seasons for touring cycling in Pinilla De Molina. Temperatures are milder, and the natural landscapes are vibrant. Summers can be very hot, and winters may bring cold temperatures and snow, especially at higher elevations, making conditions more challenging.

Q: Can I expect to find cafes or places to refuel along the routes?
A: The region is characterized by remote sections within the Alto Tajo Natural Park. While some routes may pass through small villages like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5167073" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Checa&lt;/a&gt;, opportunities for cafes or shops might be limited. It's advisable to carry sufficient water and snacks, especially on longer routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/6929948" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Checa Village – The towers of Chequilla loop from Peralejos de las Truchas&lt;/a&gt;, and plan your stops in advance.

Q: What should I wear for touring cycling in Pinilla De Molina?
A: Due to the varied terrain and potential for significant elevation changes, layered clothing is recommended. Even on warm days, temperatures can drop at higher altitudes or in shaded canyons. Essential gear includes a helmet, appropriate cycling attire, gloves, and rain gear, as weather can be unpredictable in mountainous regions. Don't forget sun protection and plenty of water.
tips: Beautiful section of road that takes in incredible rock sculptures by the road. Red rock surrounds you and gives you a sense of being in Sedona, Arizona!

Chequilla is a small and picturesque village in the province of Guadalajara, known for its impressive rocky landscape and tranquility.
The houses of Chequilla are nestled in red sandstone rock formations, creating a unique and charming landscape. Chequilla is a perfect place to disconnect and enjoy nature in a quiet and welcoming environment.
Coming from Chequilla it is a very steep and beautiful road to the Sima de las Grobias.
Incredible mountain road at 1200 masl crossing the stone bridge of the Cabrillas River surrounded by pine trees on the slopes of the mountain.
Quiet and small population surrounded by empty mountains without mobile cover, with a fountain at the entrance of it and a public laundry room with a roof landscaped with grass.

waypoints.name: Steep climb towards the Sima de las Grobias
